untranslatable_reason: Signal requires ranking stocks by 26-week historical returns and selecting top/bottom performers. This requires cross-sectional ranking logic (relative outperformance) which cannot be expressed as a single-instrument rule set. The backtest engine needs portfolio-level ranking capability, not just indicator thresholds on individual instruments.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy: used returns indicator as directional approximation. However, the core strategy is a cross-sectional momentum rank (long top quintile, short bottom quintile) rebalanced weekly over 26-week periods. This requires: (1) calculation of 26-week returns for entire universe, (2) percentile ranking across all stocks, (3) portfolio construction from ranked groups. Single-instrument conditions cannot capture this. Recommend implementing as custom ranking rule in backtest engine with parameters: lookback_weeks=26, holding_period_weeks=26, rebalance_frequency='weekly', position_type='long_short_ranked'.
